summary: The Docker build failed because a wget command to download a nydus release returned an HTTP 502 error, causing buildx bake to fail during the solve phase.
root_cause: A network/media fetch failed: wget to GitHub returned "HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error" and the wget process exited with code 1, which caused buildx bake to fail.

evidence.quote: #72 0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the download command hit a 502 Bad Gateway error from the remote server.

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Shows the wget command exited with a non-zero status, failing the build step that runs it.

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Indicates the buildkit solve step failed because the wget process failed.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: CI job reports buildx bake failure tied to the failing wget command.

evidence.quote: time="2026-05-06T19:55:11Z" level=debug msg="fetch failed" digest="sha256:0288b5a0d7e71a2cdbdf114cb9869ce2bc4b05adfd6aa5e3ee9fa4b3847e7b80" error="failed to do request: Get \"https://cdn01.quay.io/quayio-production-s3/...\": context canceled"
evidence.reason: Additional fetch failures and 'context canceled' errors show network/request problems during image layer downloads.

evidence.quote: Failed to save: Unable to reserve cache with key docker.io--tonistiigi--binfmt-latest-linux-x64, another job may be creating this cache.
evidence.reason: A cache reservation conflict appeared at the end, suggesting concurrent jobs or transient resource contention in the CI environment.

suggested_fix: Retry the CI job (network/remote-service transient failure). If failures persist, verify the URL https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/... is reachable from the runner, add retries or fallback/mirroring for the wget download in the Dockerfile, or vendor the required nydus artifact into the build context to avoid external fetches.

summary: A functional test failed because pip could not download a test package from GitHub: repeated 502 responses caused an OSError and the installation script exited with a non-zero code.
root_cause: tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] failed because pip was unable to fetch https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip from github.com due to repeated 502 responses (network error), causing an OSError and a script exit code 1.

evidence.reason: Shows the exact test that failed.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Shows pip could not download the package from github.com due to repeated 502 responses (network error).
Shows the test failed because the installation script exited with a non-zero exit code.

evidence.quote: nox > Command pytest tests/functional --verbose --numprocesses auto --showlocals --durations=15 failed with exit code 1
evidence.reason: Demonstrates the test session failed and the job returned a non-zero exit code.

suggested_fix: Retry the CI job (transient GitHub 502s often recover). If failures recur, make the test independent of external GitHub availability by hosting the test package locally or mocking the network request (or add retries/backoff in the test fixture) so the test does not fail on transient 502 responses.

summary: The Jest run ended with one failing test suite (exit code 1). Logs contain a runtime error "Error: Missing ShellJS command name" and a warning about an unresolved environment variable in src/getConfig.ts.
root_cause: A runtime test failure caused Jest to exit with code 1; logs include "Error: Missing ShellJS command name" and a warning from src/getConfig.ts that "Failed to replace env in config: ${ENV_VAR_123}", indicating a test encountered missing ShellJS input and/or an unset environment variable.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Shows that the test run had a failing test suite causing the job to fail.
Indicates the pnpm test script (jest) failed and terminated with a non-zero exit.
A runtime error present in the logs which likely caused a test to fail during execution.

evidence.quote: [WARN] Failed to replace env in config: ${ENV_VAR_123}
evidence.reason: Shows an environment variable replacement failed during tests, which could contribute to the failing behavior.
Identifies the source file and location where the environment variable replacement warning was emitted.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the failing test locally to get the failing test name and stack trace. Inspect the test that triggers "Missing ShellJS command name" and ensure ShellJS is invoked with a command name. Also ensure ENV_VAR_123 is defined in the CI environment or update src/getConfig.ts to handle missing env vars (provide a default or throw a clearer error).
